Aadhaar Photo Resize

Resize and compress your photo for Aadhaar update and enrolment portal uploads. Select a preset or enter a custom size. Your photo never leaves your device.

No Signup
Browser-based
Free to use
Privacy-first
Files stay on your device. Not uploaded anywhere.

This is a general image resize tool. JaldiDocs is not affiliated with UIDAI or the Aadhaar programme. Please verify the exact photo requirements on the official UIDAI portal (uidai.gov.in) before uploading. Do not enter your Aadhaar number in this tool.

Drop your photo here or click to choose

JPG, PNG or WEBP • Colour photo, plain background

Max 15MB per file

Advertisement

Ad space reserved for future

Advertisement space

Ad space reserved for future

Photo Requirements for Aadhaar

For Aadhaar enrolment and update, UIDAI requires a clear colour photograph with both eyes open. The background should be plain white or light-coloured. Glasses, caps and face coverings are generally not allowed. The face should be clearly visible and centred.

How to Resize Your Photo for Aadhaar

  1. Upload a clear, recent photo with a plain background
  2. Select a preset size or enter custom dimensions
  3. Set a target file size in KB if the portal has a limit
  4. Click "Resize Photo" and download the result
  5. Verify it meets the portal's requirements before uploading

Important Disclaimer

JaldiDocs is an independent tool and is not affiliated with UIDAI, the Government of India, or any official Aadhaar service. Photo requirements can change. Always check the official UIDAI portal at uidai.gov.in for the latest specifications. Do not enter your Aadhaar number or any personal ID details in this tool.

Practical guidance for Aadhaar Photo Resize

Resize your photo for Aadhaar card update, enrollment and other government portal requirements. Student tools are useful for exam forms, identity updates, scholarship portals, and job applications that reject files outside strict limits. JaldiDocs is designed for everyday Indian document workflows where a small mismatch in size, format, page order, or file weight can cause an upload to fail.

Before you use the tool

  • Keep the original file unchanged so you can restart if a form has a different requirement.
  • Check whether the portal asks for pixels, millimetres, page size, file format, or maximum KB/MB.
  • Use a clear file name without special symbols before uploading to government, exam, tax, or business portals.

After downloading

  • Read the latest official notification before final submission.
  • Match both dimensions and file size, because many portals validate both.
  • Keep the original file unchanged so you can make a new version if a portal rejects the first one.
  • Open the final file once on your device and confirm it is readable before submitting it anywhere.

Privacy and accuracy note

The tool runs in your browser wherever possible, which means your file is processed on your own device instead of being uploaded to JaldiDocs. Official requirements can still change, so always compare the output with the latest instructions from the website or organization where you plan to submit it.
